status: both exact length-three and lengths-two-or-three child-step greatest fixed points are empty

scope.legality: all connector vertices distinct and no collinear triple
scope.closure: a parent survives iff it has a legal ordered word all of whose child steps survive

theorem_boundary.proved_exact_length_three: There is no nonempty child-step-closed subalphabet when every connector word has exact length three.
theorem_boundary.proved_lengths_two_or_three: There is no nonempty child-step-closed subalphabet even when every parent may choose any legal connector word of length two or three.
theorem_boundary.cache_consistency: The independent enumeration gives 552 legal length-two words and the previously recorded 56,516 legal length-three words without loading connector_domains4.pkl.
theorem_boundary.tail_consequence: Any child-step-closed policy for this radius-two menu and matrix must use a length-four or length-five connector in its recurrent dependency core; the hoped-for uniformly low-branching length-at-most-three policy is impossible.
theorem_boundary.not_implied: This does not rule out a mixed-length policy using length-four or length-five words whose incidence matrix has Perron-Frobenius growth exactly three, and it does not prove connector availability, a contracting far-secant transfer, or an unconditional theorem.
theorem_boundary.remaining_obstruction: Longer words restore larger birth cohorts, while exact-zero residual lines and global old-old secants retain Boolean-strength poison. They still require a promoted finite state or a separate reachable-tail argument.
